steps.action: Repeat the payment proposal after correcting bank determination
steps.reason: A successful proposal confirms the bank can now be determined.

title: Investigate Missing Payment Medium Generation
description: How to diagnose payment runs that create payment documents but do not generate the expected bank file or payment medium output.

steps.action: Confirm the payment run created payment documents successfully
steps.reason: If payment documents do not exist, the issue is earlier than payment medium generation.

steps.action: Check whether payment medium creation is included in the run setup
steps.reason: Not every run variant is configured to produce a file automatically.

steps.action: Review payment medium format and output path configuration
steps.reason: Missing format, path, or bank setup often prevents file creation.

steps.action: Check logs for formatting or output generation errors
steps.reason: The payment run may complete while the output generation fails separately.

steps.action: Regenerate and verify the payment medium after correction
steps.reason: The final step confirms the bank file is now produced as expected.

title: Investigate a Cost Center Missing from the Standard Hierarchy
description: How to diagnose reporting or master-data problems caused by incorrect standard hierarchy assignment.

steps.action: Confirm the cost center exists and is valid
steps.reason: Hierarchy issues should not be analyzed until the basic master record is confirmed.

steps.action: Review the standard hierarchy and search for the cost center
steps.reason: This shows whether the cost center is missing entirely or assigned to the wrong node.

steps.action: Check whether the hierarchy problem affects posting, reporting, or both
steps.reason: The business impact helps determine urgency and correction path.

steps.action: Correct the hierarchy assignment following controlling governance
steps.reason: Standard hierarchy corrections should be done carefully because they affect broad reporting structures.

steps.action: Validate the corrected hierarchy placement
steps.reason: A final check confirms the cost center now appears in the right place.

title: Investigate an Internal Order Blocked by Status
description: How to diagnose why an internal order cannot accept postings because of system or user status.

steps.action: Display the internal order and review system and user statuses
steps.reason: Status is the most common reason an otherwise valid order rejects postings.

steps.action: Check whether the order is closed, technically complete, or blocked for actual postings
steps.reason: Different statuses have different business meanings and correction paths.

steps.action: Confirm the posting date and business purpose are still valid
steps.reason: Even a technically open order may no longer be valid for the intended use.

steps.action: Adjust the order status only if business approval exists
steps.reason: Status changes can reopen financial posting and should follow governance.

steps.action: Retest the blocked posting afterwards
steps.reason: A successful retest confirms the order can now accept the transaction.

title: Investigate a WBS Element Blocked by Status
description: How to diagnose project postings or logistics steps that fail because a WBS element is not valid for posting.

steps.action: Display the WBS element and review its current statuses
steps.reason: The project status determines whether postings, procurement, or settlement are allowed.

steps.action: Check whether the WBS is released and not blocked by user status
steps.reason: User status profiles often add extra business restrictions beyond the basic system status.

steps.action: Review whether closure or settlement status is blocking the scenario
steps.reason: A WBS may be valid historically but no longer open for new activity.

steps.action: Correct the status only if approved by the project team
steps.reason: Project status changes affect more than a single posting and should be controlled.

steps.action: Retry the original process after status correction
steps.reason: A final retest confirms the WBS can now be used correctly.

title: Investigate a Maintenance Order Not Released
description: How to diagnose a maintenance order that exists but cannot proceed because release has not happened.

steps.action: Display the maintenance order and check its current status
steps.reason: The first step is to confirm that the order is truly not released and not blocked for another reason.

steps.action: Review whether planning, data completeness, or approval dependencies are missing
steps.reason: Orders often remain unreleased because upstream preparation is incomplete.

steps.action: Complete any missing planning or master data fields
steps.reason: The order must be complete enough to support release and downstream execution.

steps.action: Release the order if authorized and appropriate
steps.reason: Once the prerequisites are complete, release should make the order available for execution.

steps.action: Recheck the order status and blocked process afterwards
steps.reason: A final check confirms the order is now usable for confirmation, settlement, or other follow-on steps.

title: Investigate an Incomplete Maintenance Notification
description: How to diagnose maintenance notifications that are missing required malfunction, cause, or technical object data.

steps.action: Display the notification and review missing or incomplete sections
steps.reason: This shows which required fields are preventing proper follow-on processing.

steps.action: Check malfunction data, coding, cause, and technical object assignment
steps.reason: Most PM notification problems come from missing issue details or object references.

steps.action: Maintain the missing notification data
steps.reason: The correction should be made directly in the notification if business rules allow it.

steps.action: Review whether the incomplete notification blocked order creation or analysis
steps.reason: Understanding the impact helps confirm the right fix was applied.

steps.action: Validate the notification status after correction
steps.reason: A final check confirms the notification is now complete enough for downstream use.

title: Investigate Incorrect Technical Object Assignment
description: How to diagnose equipment and functional location hierarchy issues in Plant Maintenance.

steps.action: Display the equipment and review its current installation and assignment
steps.reason: This confirms where SAP currently thinks the equipment is installed.

steps.action: Review the target functional location and hierarchy
steps.reason: You need to compare the actual versus expected structure to identify the mismatch.

steps.action: Determine whether the issue came from master data, movement, or manual maintenance
steps.reason: The correction path depends on how the wrong assignment happened.

steps.action: Correct the installation or assignment using the proper technical object process
steps.reason: The technical object should be corrected through controlled maintenance rather than workaround postings.

steps.action: Validate hierarchy and downstream PM behavior afterwards
steps.reason: A final check confirms the object now appears correctly in structure and related processes.

title: Investigate Payroll Control Record Blocking HR Changes
description: How to diagnose HR master data changes that are blocked because of payroll control record status.

steps.action: Identify the employee's payroll area and the blocked HR change
steps.reason: Payroll control record logic is driven by payroll area and current processing phase.

steps.action: Review the payroll control record status
steps.reason: This shows whether changes are blocked due to release, correction, or payroll execution phase.

steps.action: Confirm whether the change should wait for payroll completion or be done in correction phase
steps.reason: Not all blocked changes should be forced through immediately.

steps.action: Coordinate with payroll administration before changing payroll status
steps.reason: Payroll control record changes affect broad payroll processing and should be controlled.

steps.action: Retry the HR master data maintenance when the payroll status allows it
steps.reason: A successful retry confirms the block was due to payroll control timing.

title: Investigate an HR Infotype Time Constraint Error
description: How to diagnose overlapping or invalid HR infotype records that violate time constraints.

steps.action: Display existing infotype records and validity periods
steps.reason: You need to see the current time slices before changing anything.

steps.action: Identify the conflicting begin and end dates
steps.reason: The overlap problem is usually obvious once the full validity history is visible.

steps.action: Check the relevant infotype's time constraint behavior
steps.reason: Different infotypes allow different overlap patterns.

steps.action: Delimit or correct the old record before inserting the new one
steps.reason: The conflict must be resolved in the time slices before the new record can save successfully.

steps.action: Validate the final infotype history after the change
steps.reason: A final review confirms the record history is now clean and complete.

title: Investigate a Missing Holder Relationship in OM
description: How to diagnose why an employee is not shown as holder of a position in Organizational Management.

steps.action: Review the org unit and position where the employee should appear
steps.reason: This confirms the expected structural placement in OM.

steps.action: Check the employee's organizational assignment in HR master data
steps.reason: PA master data and OM should align; mismatches often explain visibility problems.

steps.action: Display the org structure and verify whether the holder relationship exists
steps.reason: This helps distinguish a display issue from a true missing OM relationship.

steps.action: Correct the holder relationship if it is missing or wrong
steps.reason: The structural relationship must exist correctly for workflow and OM reporting to work.

steps.action: Validate the final structure after the correction
steps.reason: A final check confirms the employee now appears correctly in OM.

title: Create a Standard Sales Order
description: End-to-end flow for creating a standard sales order and validating the core commercial data before delivery processing begins.

steps.action: Enter the order type, sales area, and sold-to party
steps.reason: These values define the commercial context and drive partner, pricing, and document behavior.

steps.action: Add the required materials, quantities, and requested delivery dates
steps.reason: This is the core commercial content of the order and drives ATP, delivery, and billing later.

steps.action: Review pricing, partners, and shipping data
steps.reason: Before saving, confirm SAP determined the correct price, ship-to, payer, and shipping conditions.

steps.action: Save the sales order and note the document number
steps.reason: The order number is needed for delivery creation, billing, and customer follow-up.

steps.action: Display the saved order and verify the status overview
steps.reason: A quick validation confirms the order saved correctly and is ready for downstream processing.

title: Create an Outbound Delivery
description: Standard process for creating an outbound delivery from a sales order once the order is due for shipping.

steps.action: Enter the shipping point and sales order number
steps.reason: Delivery creation uses the shipping point and source document to build the logistics document.

steps.action: Review due quantities, delivery date, and item selection
steps.reason: You need to confirm that the correct order items and quantities are being proposed.

steps.action: Save the outbound delivery
steps.reason: Saving creates the logistics document used for picking, packing, and goods issue.

steps.action: Display the delivery and review shipping data
steps.reason: This confirms the delivery was created correctly with the expected ship-to and item content.

title: Post Goods Issue for an Outbound Delivery
description: Standard process for completing the warehouse-to-shipping step by posting goods issue on an outbound delivery.

steps.action: Open the outbound delivery and review picked quantities
steps.reason: Goods issue should only be posted after delivery quantities and picking status are correct.

steps.action: Check batches, storage location, and shipping details if relevant
steps.reason: These logistics details directly affect stock posting and traceability.

steps.action: Post goods issue
steps.reason: PGI reduces stock, updates document flow, and allows billing to continue.

steps.action: Re-display the delivery and confirm goods issue status
steps.reason: A final check confirms the delivery moved into the next logistics stage.

title: Create a Billing Document
description: Standard process for creating an invoice or other billing document from a delivery or sales document.

steps.action: Enter the source delivery or sales document number
steps.reason: Billing is usually created with reference to an earlier logistics or sales document.

steps.action: Review billing type, payer, and amounts
steps.reason: This confirms that SAP selected the correct invoice context and values.

steps.action: Save the billing document
steps.reason: Saving creates the invoice and, depending on setup, triggers accounting posting.

steps.action: Display the billing document and validate header and item data
steps.reason: This confirms the invoice was created as expected and is available for follow-up.

title: Create a Purchase Requisition
description: Standard process for creating a purchase requisition for materials or services before sourcing and PO creation.

steps.action: Enter material or service details, quantity, plant, and delivery date
steps.reason: These fields define what the business needs and when it is required.

steps.action: Maintain account assignment or source details if required
steps.reason: Many requisitions require cost objects, WBS elements, or preferred procurement context.

steps.action: Check item overview and save the requisition
steps.reason: Saving creates the PR that can later be approved and converted into a PO.

steps.action: Display the requisition and confirm the document data
steps.reason: A quick review confirms the PR is complete and ready for release or conversion.

title: Convert a Purchase Requisition into a Purchase Order
description: Standard process for converting an approved purchase requisition into a purchase order.

steps.action: Start a purchase order and adopt the purchase requisition reference
steps.reason: Referencing the PR carries forward required material and procurement data.

steps.action: Enter or confirm vendor, purchasing organization, and pricing details
steps.reason: The PO needs the correct commercial and sourcing information before save.

steps.action: Review item details, delivery date, and account assignment
steps.reason: This ensures the PO is consistent before committing it.

steps.action: Save the purchase order
steps.reason: Saving creates the PO that vendors and logistics processes will use.

steps.action: Display the PO and confirm header and item content
steps.reason: A final review confirms the purchase order is correct and available for release or follow-on processing.

title: Create a Purchase Order Directly
description: Standard process for creating a purchase order directly without first creating a requisition.

steps.action: Enter vendor, purchasing organization, company code, and purchasing group
steps.reason: These values define the commercial ownership and supplier context of the PO.

steps.action: Add items with material, quantity, plant, delivery date, and price
steps.reason: These fields define what is being purchased and under what delivery conditions.

steps.action: Maintain account assignment, confirmations, or texts if needed
steps.reason: Many purchasing scenarios require more than the minimum basic fields.

steps.action: Save the purchase order
steps.reason: The saved PO becomes the main procurement and logistics reference document.

steps.action: Display and validate the saved PO
steps.reason: The final review confirms the PO was created with the right terms and item content.

title: Post a Vendor Invoice
description: Standard Logistics Invoice Verification process for entering a vendor invoice against a purchase order.

steps.action: Enter invoice header data such as vendor, invoice date, amount, and reference
steps.reason: The header defines the legal and commercial context of the invoice.

steps.action: Reference the purchase order and pull in the expected items
steps.reason: Referencing the PO allows SAP to compare invoice values against procurement history.

steps.action: Review quantities, prices, taxes, and any variances
steps.reason: This step prevents avoidable invoice block situations and data inconsistencies.

steps.action: Post the invoice
steps.reason: Posting creates the vendor invoice and updates PO history.

steps.action: Review the vendor open item if needed
steps.reason: A final check confirms the invoice is now posted and visible in AP.

title: Run an Automatic Payment Proposal and Payment Run
description: Standard process for executing the F110 automatic payment program for vendor payments.

steps.action: Create or open the payment run parameters
steps.reason: The payment run is controlled by dates, company codes, payment methods, and account ranges.

steps.action: Maintain the proposal parameters and save
steps.reason: These settings determine which open items are eligible for payment selection.

steps.action: Run the payment proposal and review the proposal log
steps.reason: The proposal stage allows validation before actual payment posting.

steps.action: Execute the payment run once the proposal is accepted
steps.reason: This creates the payment documents and prepares or generates payment media depending on configuration.

steps.action: Check that paid vendor items were cleared
steps.reason: A final check confirms the payment run had the expected business result.

title: Post an Incoming Customer Payment
description: Standard process for posting an incoming payment from a customer and clearing open receivables.

steps.action: Enter customer, company code, bank data, and payment amount
steps.reason: These values define the incoming payment context and accounting entry.

steps.action: Select the open items to clear
steps.reason: The key business action is matching the payment against the correct receivables.

steps.action: Review residuals, differences, or partial payment handling if needed
steps.reason: Not every payment matches invoices exactly, so this step matters in real life.

steps.action: Post the incoming payment
steps.reason: Posting clears the customer item and updates accounting balances.

steps.action: Review the customer account afterwards
steps.reason: This confirms the receivable is cleared or updated as expected.

title: Create a New Material Master
description: Standard process for creating a new material with the required views and organizational levels.

steps.action: Enter the material type, industry sector, and reference if used
steps.reason: These values define the base structure and behavior of the new material.

steps.action: Select the required views and organizational levels
steps.reason: The material is only usable where the relevant views are maintained.

steps.action: Maintain basic data, purchasing, sales, MRP, and accounting views as required
steps.reason: Different business processes depend on different sections of the material master.

steps.action: Save the new material
steps.reason: Saving creates the material master record and makes it available for downstream use.

steps.action: Display the material and verify key views
steps.reason: A final validation confirms the material is complete enough for business use.

title: Extend a Material to a New Plant or View
description: Standard process for extending an existing material to additional organizational levels or views.

steps.action: Enter the existing material number and choose the additional views to extend
steps.reason: MM01 is also used for extension, not only for brand-new materials.

steps.action: Enter the new organizational levels such as plant, storage location, or sales area
steps.reason: The extension is only valid in the org levels you specify here.

steps.action: Maintain the required new data for the added views
steps.reason: Each new view requires enough data to support the intended process.

steps.action: Save the extension
steps.reason: This makes the material usable in the newly added organizational context.

steps.action: Display the material in the new org level and verify the data
steps.reason: A quick review confirms the extension was successful.

title: Create a Customer Business Partner
description: Standard process for creating a new customer business partner with the required roles and core master data.

steps.action: Choose the correct BP category and customer role
steps.reason: The role selection controls which data sections are available and required.

steps.action: Maintain general data such as name, address, and communication details
steps.reason: These are the core identity fields used across processes.

steps.action: Maintain company code and sales area data where required
steps.reason: Customer billing, dunning, sales, and shipping all rely on org-specific BP data.

steps.action: Save the business partner
steps.reason: Saving creates the master record and enables business transactions.

steps.action: Re-display the BP and verify roles and organizational data
steps.reason: A final validation confirms the partner is ready for use in SD and FI.

title: Create a Supplier Business Partner
description: Standard process for creating a supplier business partner with purchasing and accounting roles.

steps.action: Choose the correct BP category and supplier roles
steps.reason: Supplier creation depends on the right role setup for purchasing and accounting.

steps.action: Maintain general identification, address, and communication data
steps.reason: These core fields are shared across procurement and finance usage.

steps.action: Maintain company code, purchasing, and bank details as required
steps.reason: Without org-specific supplier data, procurement and payment processes will fail later.

steps.action: Save the supplier business partner
steps.reason: Saving creates the master data needed for purchasing and AP processing.

steps.action: Validate the supplier roles and key data after save
steps.reason: A final check ensures the supplier is usable in day-to-day procurement.

title: Post a Stock Transfer or Transfer Posting
description: Standard process for moving stock between storage locations, plants, or stock types using transfer posting logic.

steps.action: Choose transfer posting and the correct movement scenario
steps.reason: The movement type and posting category determine how stock and valuation are updated.

steps.action: Enter material, quantity, source, and target locations
steps.reason: These details define exactly which stock is moving and where it is going.

steps.action: Review batches, special stock, or stock type if relevant
steps.reason: Stock movements often depend on more than just unrestricted-use quantity.

steps.action: Post the transfer
steps.reason: Posting updates inventory and creates the material document.

steps.action: Check stock after posting
steps.reason: A final stock check confirms the transfer had the intended result.

title: Perform a Physical Inventory Count
description: Standard process for counting stock and posting differences through physical inventory.

steps.action: Create the physical inventory document
steps.reason: The inventory document defines which materials and locations are being counted.

steps.action: Enter the counted quantities
steps.reason: This captures the real physical stock found during the count.

steps.action: Review the differences and post inventory adjustments
steps.reason: Posting differences updates system stock to match the counted result.

steps.action: Check stock balances after the posting
steps.reason: A final check confirms the inventory update was successful.

title: Create a Production Order
description: Standard process for creating a production order for a material in a plant.

steps.action: Enter order type, material, plant, and quantity
steps.reason: These values define what is being produced and in which production context.

steps.action: Review the proposed BOM, routing, and dates
steps.reason: The production order relies on task list and component determination to build the process.

steps.action: Save the production order
steps.reason: Saving creates the order that can then be released and processed on the shop floor.

steps.action: Display the saved order and check components and operations
steps.reason: A final check confirms the order was created with the expected production structure.

title: Release a Production Order
description: Standard process for releasing a production order so that execution can begin.

steps.action: Open the production order in change mode
steps.reason: Order release is a status change performed on the production order itself.

steps.action: Review component availability and order readiness
steps.reason: It is good practice to ensure the order is ready before release.

steps.action: Release the order
steps.reason: Release enables execution-related steps such as confirmations and goods movements.

steps.action: Confirm the released status
steps.reason: A quick review confirms the order moved into executable status.

title: Confirm a Production Order Operation
description: Standard process for posting production order confirmation for yield, time, and progress.

steps.action: Enter the production order or operation reference
steps.reason: This identifies the operation or phase being confirmed.

steps.action: Maintain yield, scrap, time, and final confirmation indicators as needed
steps.reason: These are the core execution data points captured during confirmation.

steps.action: Save the confirmation
steps.reason: Saving updates order progress and may trigger backflush or goods receipt depending on setup.

steps.action: Review updated order status afterwards
steps.reason: A final check confirms the confirmation affected the order as expected.

title: Create a Service Entry Sheet
description: Standard process for recording and accepting services performed against a service purchase order.

steps.action: Create a new service entry sheet with reference to the PO
steps.reason: Referencing the PO ensures the service lines and limits come from the approved procurement document.

steps.action: Enter the performed service quantities and values
steps.reason: This records what was actually delivered by the vendor.

steps.action: Check account assignment and service acceptance data
steps.reason: Service acceptance depends on both quantity correctness and business approval context.

steps.action: Save the service entry sheet
steps.reason: Saving creates the SES for later acceptance and invoice processing.

steps.action: Accept the SES if your process allows immediate acceptance
steps.reason: Acceptance makes the service available for follow-on invoice verification.

title: Create a Maintenance Notification
description: Standard process for recording a maintenance issue, malfunction, or technical problem for follow-up.

steps.action: Choose the notification type and technical object
steps.reason: The notification type and object determine the business context and later follow-on processes.

steps.action: Enter malfunction, damage, and description details
steps.reason: Clear issue details are essential for later maintenance planning and analysis.

steps.action: Maintain priority and any initial coding if required
steps.reason: Priority and coding support planning, reporting, and escalation.

steps.action: Save the notification
steps.reason: Saving creates the maintenance case and enables follow-on action such as order creation.

steps.action: Display and verify the notification
steps.reason: A final review confirms the notification was created with usable maintenance data.

title: Create a Maintenance Order
description: Standard process for creating a maintenance order for work execution in Plant Maintenance.

steps.action: Enter order type, planning plant, and technical object or notification reference
steps.reason: These fields define the maintenance context and link the order to the right asset or issue.

steps.action: Maintain operations, work center, and required dates
steps.reason: This is the execution plan that the maintenance team will follow.

steps.action: Add components, services, or account assignments if needed
steps.reason: Real maintenance orders often need materials and cost assignments before release.

steps.action: Save the maintenance order
steps.reason: Saving creates the PM order so it can be released and processed.

steps.action: Display and validate the order data
steps.reason: A final review confirms the order is correctly structured for execution.

title: Create an Internal Order
description: Standard controlling process for creating an internal order for cost collection and monitoring.

steps.action: Enter the order type, controlling area, and description
steps.reason: The order type determines number range, status behavior, and settlement logic.

steps.action: Maintain responsible cost center, company code, and dates
steps.reason: These fields define ownership and the validity context of the order.

steps.action: Maintain settlement rule if required at creation time
steps.reason: Many internal orders eventually need settlement and this can be prepared early.

steps.action: Save the internal order
steps.reason: Saving creates the order for later postings and cost monitoring.

steps.action: Display and validate the order afterwards
steps.reason: A quick check confirms the internal order is ready for operational use.

title: Settle an Internal Order
description: Standard process for settling collected costs from an internal order to its receiver.

steps.action: Review the internal order and confirm costs and receiver setup
steps.reason: Before settlement, confirm the order actually holds costs and has the right settlement logic.

steps.action: Enter the internal order and settlement period
steps.reason: Settlement is executed for a defined period and controlling context.

steps.action: Run settlement
steps.reason: This transfers the collected values to the defined settlement receiver.

steps.action: Review the order after settlement
steps.reason: A final check confirms settlement status and cost movement were updated.

title: Create a Cost Center
description: Standard process for creating a new cost center in Controlling.

steps.action: Enter controlling area, cost center code, and validity dates
steps.reason: These fields define the identity and usable life of the cost center.

steps.action: Maintain name, responsible person, hierarchy assignment, and category
steps.reason: These fields are needed for reporting, ownership, and structural placement.

steps.action: Maintain company code and business area context if required
steps.reason: Organizational alignment matters for finance and controlling integration.

steps.action: Save the cost center
steps.reason: Saving creates the master record for future postings and reporting.

steps.action: Display and validate the created cost center
steps.reason: A final review confirms the master data is usable and in the correct hierarchy.

title: Create a Customer Quotation
description: Standard process for creating a quotation in the sales cycle before order entry.

steps.action: Enter quotation type, sales area, and sold-to party
steps.reason: These values define the pre-sales document context.

steps.action: Add materials, quantities, requested dates, and pricing
steps.reason: The quotation needs to reflect the commercial offer being made.

steps.action: Review validity dates and partner data
steps.reason: Quotations are only useful when they are valid for the intended time window and customer setup.

steps.action: Save the quotation
steps.reason: Saving creates the sales offer document for later reference or conversion.

steps.action: Display and validate the quotation
